front: What is an orthogonal set of vectors?
back: A set of vectors {v₁, v₂, ..., vₖ} in an inner product space V is orthogonal if ⟨vᵢ, vⱼ⟩ = 0 for all i ≠ j. Every pair of distinct vectors in the set is orthogonal.

front: What is the key property of an orthogonal set of nonzero vectors?
back: An orthogonal set of nonzero vectors is linearly independent. This makes them an excellent candidate for forming a basis.
An orthonormal set is an orthogonal set where each vector has unit length (i.e., ‖v‖ = 1 for every vector v in the set). It satisfies ⟨vᵢ, vⱼ⟩ = 0 for i ≠ j and ⟨vᵢ, vᵢ⟩ = 1.
An orthogonal basis for a subspace W is a basis for W that is also an orthogonal set. An orthonormal basis is an orthogonal basis where each basis vector has unit length.

front: State the advantage of representing a vector with coordinates relative to an orthogonal basis.
back: If B = {b₁, b₂, ..., bₖ} is an orthogonal basis for a subspace W, then the coordinate (or weight) for a vector y in W is given by cⱼ = ⟨y, bⱼ⟩ / ⟨bⱼ, bⱼ⟩. This is simpler than solving a system of equations, which is required for a non-orthogonal basis.
